title: CSS Foundations
content:
## 🎨 CSS Foundations: Making the Web Beautiful CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) controls how HTML looks. To master it, you must understand its core logic — not just memorize properties. This lesson covers the fundamental concepts that make CSS predictable. --- ## 1️⃣ The Box Model Every HTML element is a rectangular **box**. The box model has four layers (from inside out): ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐ margin (space outside border) │ ┌───────────────────────────────────────┐ │ border (the line) │ │ ┌─────────────────────────────────┐ │ │ padding (space inside border) │ │ │ CONTENT │ │ │ content (text, image) │ │ └─────────────────────────────────┘ │ │ │ └───────────────────────────────────────┘ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ```css .card { width: 300px; /* Content width */ padding: 20px; /* Space inside border */ border: 2px solid #000; margin: 10px; /* Space outside border */ } ``` **`box-sizing: border-box`** — Makes `width` include padding and border, not just content. Always set this globally: ```css *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; } ``` --- ## 2️⃣ Selectors | Selector | Example | Targets | |----------|---------|---------| | Element | `h1` | All `

` tags | | Class | `.card` | All elements with `class="card"` | | ID | `#header` | ONE element with `id="header"` | | Descendant | `div p` | All `

` inside `

` | | Direct Child | `ul > li` | `
  • ` that is direct child of `
      ` | | Adjacent Sibling | `h1 + p` | `

      ` immediately after `

      ` | | Pseudo-class | `a:hover` | `` when mouse hovers | | Pseudo-element | `p::first-line` | First line of `

      ` | | Attribute | `[type="email"]` | Elements with that attribute | | Universal | `*` | ALL elements | --- ## 3️⃣ Specificity & The Cascade When multiple rules target the same element, **specificity** determines which wins: | Selector Type | Specificity Points | |---|---| | Inline `style=""` | 1000 | | `#id` | 100 | | `.class`, `[attr]`, `:pseudo-class` | 10 | | `element`, `::pseudo-element` | 1 | | `*` | 0 | | `!important` | Overrides everything (avoid using) | **The Cascade**: If specificity is equal, the **last rule in the file wins**. --- ## 4️⃣ Colors ```css /* Hexadecimal */ color: #ff6b35; /* RGB */ color: rgb(255, 107, 53); /* RGBA (with transparency: 0-1) */ color: rgba(255, 107, 53, 0.5); /* HSL (Hue: 0-360, Saturation: %, Lightness: %) */ color: hsl(200, 100%, 50%); /* HSL is intuitive: rotate hue to change color, adjust lightness */ ``` --- ## 5️⃣ Units | Unit | Description | Best For | |------|-------------|---------| | `px` | Fixed pixels | Borders, shadows | | `rem` | Relative to root font-size (default 16px) | Font sizes, spacing | | `em` | Relative to parent font-size | Component-scoped spacing | | `%` | Relative to parent element | Widths, heights | | `vw` | 1% of viewport width | Full-width elements | | `vh` | 1% of viewport height | Full-height sections | | `clamp(min, pref, max)` | Fluid value within bounds | Responsive typography | --- ## 6️⃣ Positioning ```css position: static; /* Default — normal document flow */ position: relative; /* Moved relative to its normal position */ position: absolute; /* Removed from flow, positioned to nearest positioned ancestor */ position: fixed; /* Stays on screen when scrolling (fixed to viewport) */ position: sticky; /* Scrolls normally until it hits a threshold, then sticks */ ``` `z-index` controls stack order for positioned elements (higher = on top). --- ## 7️⃣ Display Values | `display` Value | Behavior | |---|---| | `block` | Full width, starts on new line (div, h1, p) | | `inline` | Only as wide as content, no width/height (span, a) | | `inline-block` | Like inline but respects width/height | | `none` | Removed from layout (invisible, takes no space) | | `flex` | Enables Flexbox | | `grid` | Enables CSS Grid | --- ## 8️⃣ `display: none` vs `visibility: hidden` | | `display: none` | `visibility: hidden` | |---|---|---| | Visible | ❌ | ❌ | | Takes up space | ❌ | ✅ | | In DOM | ✅ | ✅ | --- ## 9️⃣ Pseudo-Classes & Pseudo-Elements ```css a:hover { color: blue; } /* When mouse is over element */ a:active { color: red; } /* When element is being clicked */ input:focus { outline: 2px solid blue; } /* When element has keyboard focus */ li:nth-child(2) { ... } /* Second list item */ li:last-child { ... } /* Last list item */ p::first-line { ... } /* First line of paragraph */ h1::before { content: "★ "; } /* Insert content before element */ ``` --- ## 🔟 CSS Variables (Custom Properties) ```css :root { --primary-color: #6366f1; --font-size-base: 1rem; } .button { background: var(--primary-color); font-size: var(--font-size-base); } ``` Variables make themes and changes much easier. --- ## 💡 Summary | Concept | Key Point | |---|---| | Box Model | Content → Padding → Border → Margin | | Specificity | Inline > ID > Class > Element | | Cascade | Last rule wins (if equal specificity) | | `rem` | Best unit for fonts (scales with accessibility settings) | | `position: absolute` | Relative to nearest positioned ancestor | | `display: none` | Removes from layout entirely | | `!important` | Overrides everything — avoid it |

questions.explanation: Cascading refers to the way styles are applied based on priority and order.

questions.question_text: Which property is used to change the background color of an element?
questions.explanation: background-color is the standard CSS property for setting background fills.

questions.question_text: What are the four parts of the CSS Box Model (from inside to outside)?
questions.explanation: The order is: Content -> Padding (space inside) -> Border -> Margin (space outside).

questions.question_text: Which CSS selector has the highest specificity?
questions.explanation: ID selectors are more specific than classes, which are more specific than elements.

questions.question_text: How do you select an element with id 'header' in CSS?
questions.explanation: The '#' symbol is used for IDs in CSS.

questions.question_text: What does the 'position: absolute' property do?
options: Positions the element relative to its normal position
Positions the element relative to its nearest positioned ancestor
Fixed the element to the viewport
questions.explanation: If no positioned ancestor exists, it uses the document body.

questions.question_text: Which property is used to change the text color of an element?
questions.explanation: The 'color' property specifically targets the foreground text color.

questions.question_text: What is the difference between 'padding' and 'margin'?
options: Padding is outside the border; Margin is inside
Padding is inside the border; Margin is outside
Padding handles text size; Margin handles spacing
questions.explanation: Padding adds space between content and border; Margin adds space between elements.

questions.question_text: How do you make the text bold in CSS?
questions.explanation: font-weight controls the thickness of the characters.

options: The zoom level of the page
The horizontal position of an element
The stack order (layering) of positioned elements
questions.explanation: Higher z-index values appear on top of lower ones.

questions.question_text: What is the default value of the 'position' property?
questions.explanation: Static elements follow the normal document flow and are not affected by top/bottom/left/right properties.

questions.question_text: Which property is used to control the size of the text?

How do you apply a style to all

elements inside a

?
questions.explanation: The descendant selector (space) targets all matching nested elements.

questions.question_text: What does the 'display: none' property do?
options: Hides the element and removes it from the document layout
Hides the element but keeps its space in the layout
Moves the element to the bottom
questions.explanation: Contrast this with 'visibility: hidden', which keeps the layout space.

questions.question_text: Which CSS unit is relative to the font-size of the root element (html)?
